description_long: This bungalow features a long, rectangular structure with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in dark, weathered shingles. The roof is adorned with prominent wooden crossbeams and decorative dragon-head finials at each end, reminiscent of traditional Scandinavian or Viking architecture. The exterior walls are clad in wooden planks, giving the building a rustic and historic appearance. The eaves extend outward, providing some shelter along the sides, but there is no visible porch or veranda. The building sits on short, sturdy wooden posts, elevating it slightly above the ground. There are no visible windows or doors on the main facades, emphasizing a fortress-like, enclosed design.
description: A long, rustic wooden bungalow with a steep gable roof, decorative crossbeams, and dragon-head finials, evoking traditional Scandinavian architecture. The structure is elevated on short posts and lacks visible windows or a porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, shingled gable roof and the decorative wooden crossbeam with dragon-head finials; no windows or doors are visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steep pitch of the roof, the extended eaves, and the prominent crossbeams at the roof's peak.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the same gable roof, crossbeam, and lack of visible openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the steep roof, extended eaves, and decorative roof details.
